tEn: RewardDance: Reward Scaling in Visual Generation
tZh: RewardDance：视觉生成中的奖励缩放
sEn: RewardDance proposes a generative reward paradigm that aligns with VLM architectures, enabling scalable reward models up to 26B parameters and resolving reward hacking in visual generation.
sZh: RewardDance提出了一种与视觉语言模型架构对齐的生成式奖励范式，实现了高达260亿参数的可扩展奖励模型，并解决了视觉生成中的奖励破解问题。
absEn: Reward Models (RMs) are critical for improving generation models via Reinforcement Learning (RL), yet the RM scaling paradigm in visual generation remains largely unexplored. It primarily due to fundamental limitations in existing approaches: CLIP-based RMs suffer from architectural and input modality constraints, while prevalent Bradley-Terry losses are fundamentally misaligned with the next-token prediction mechanism of Vision-Language Models (VLMs), hindering effective scaling. More critically, the RLHF optimization process is plagued by Reward Hacking issue, where models exploit flaws in the reward signal without improving true quality. To address these challenges, we introduce RewardDance, a scalable reward modeling framework that overcomes these barriers through a novel generative reward paradigm. By reformulating the reward score as the model's probability of predicting a "yes" token, indicating that the generated image outperforms a reference image according to specific criteria, RewardDance intrinsically aligns reward objectives with VLM architectures. This alignment unlocks scaling across two dimensions: (1) Model Scaling: Systematic scaling of RMs up to 26 billion parameters; (2) Context Scaling: Integration of task-specific instructions, reference examples, and chain-of-thought (CoT) reasoning. Extensive experiments demonstrate that RewardDance significantly surpasses state-of-the-art methods in text-to-image, text-to-video, and image-to-video generation. Crucially, we resolve the persistent challenge of "reward hacking": Our large-scale RMs exhibit and maintain high reward variance during RL fine-tuning, proving their resistance to hacking and ability to produce diverse, high-quality outputs. It greatly relieves the mode collapse problem that plagues smaller models.
absZh: 奖励模型对于通过强化学习改进生成模型至关重要，然而视觉生成中的奖励模型缩放范式仍未被充分探索。这主要源于现有方法的基本限制：基于CLIP的奖励模型受限于架构和输入模态，而广泛使用的Bradley-Terry损失与视觉语言模型的下一个标记预测机制根本不对齐，阻碍了有效缩放。更关键的是，RLHF优化过程受到奖励破解问题的困扰，模型利用奖励信号的缺陷而不提升真实质量。为了解决这些挑战，我们引入了RewardDance，一个可扩展的奖励建模框架，通过一种新颖的生成式奖励范式克服了这些障碍。通过将奖励分数重新定义为模型预测“是”标记的概率（表示生成的图像根据特定标准优于参考图像），RewardDance内在地将奖励目标与视觉语言模型架构对齐。这种对齐解锁了两个维度的缩放：（1）模型缩放：系统地将奖励模型扩展到260亿参数；（2）上下文缩放：集成任务特定指令、参考示例和思维链推理。大量实验表明，RewardDance在文本到图像、文本到视频和图像到视频生成中显著超越了最先进的方法。关键的是，我们解决了“奖励破解”这一持续挑战：我们的大规模奖励模型在强化学习微调期间表现出并保持高奖励方差，证明了其抵抗破解的能力以及产生多样化、高质量输出的能力。这极大地缓解了困扰较小模型的模式崩溃问题。

contrib.en: Proposes RewardDance, a generative reward modeling framework that aligns reward objectives with VLM architectures via next-token prediction.
contrib.zh: 提出RewardDance，一种生成式奖励建模框架，通过下一个词预测将奖励目标与VLM架构对齐。

contrib.en: Demonstrates systematic scaling of reward models up to 26B parameters, showing strong positive correlation with generation quality.
contrib.zh: 展示了奖励模型系统性地扩展到260亿参数，与生成质量呈强正相关。

contrib.en: Introduces context scaling by integrating task-specific instructions, reference examples, and chain-of-thought reasoning.
contrib.zh: 引入上下文扩展，整合任务特定指令、参考示例和思维链推理。

contrib.en: Resolves reward hacking by maintaining high reward variance during RL fine-tuning with large-scale RMs.
contrib.zh: 通过大规模RM在RL微调中保持高奖励方差，解决了奖励破解问题。

contrib.en: Achieves state-of-the-art results on text-to-image, text-to-video, and image-to-video generation benchmarks.
contrib.zh: 在文本到图像、文本到视频和图像到视频生成基准上取得最先进结果。

contrib.en: Establishes scalability as a foundational principle for visual reward models, providing new insights for the field.
contrib.zh: 将可扩展性确立为视觉奖励模型的基本原则，为该领域提供新见解。

limits.en: Maximum model size explored is 26B parameters; further scaling to 70B/100B may yield additional gains.
limits.zh: 探索的最大模型规模为260亿参数；进一步扩展到700亿/1000亿可能带来额外收益。

limits.en: Focuses primarily on alignment; other dimensions like motion and aesthetics are not explored.
limits.zh: 主要关注对齐；未探索运动、美学等其他维度。

limits.en: Task scope limited to generation; understanding and editing tasks are not addressed.
limits.zh: 任务范围限于生成；未涉及理解和编辑任务。

limits.en: Multimodal scaling beyond vision (e.g., audio-to-video) is not investigated.
limits.zh: 未研究视觉之外的多模态扩展（如音频到视频）。

limits.en: Context scaling limited to instructions, references, and CoT; richer context like reflection and in-context learning remains future work.
limits.zh: 上下文扩展限于指令、参考和CoT；更丰富的上下文如反思和上下文学习留待未来工作。

items.en: Reward Models (RMs) are critical for improving generation models via Reinforcement Learning (RL), yet the RM scaling paradigm in visual generation remains largely unexplored. It primarily due to fundamental limitations in existing approaches: CLIP-based RMs suffer from architectural and input modality constraints, while prevalent Bradley-Terry losses are fundamentally misaligned with the next-token prediction mechanism of Vision-Language Models (VLMs), hindering effective scaling. More critically, the RLHF optimization process is plagued by Reward Hacking issue, where models exploit flaws in the reward signal without improving true quality. To address these challenges, we introduce RewardDance, a scalable reward modeling framework that overcomes these barriers through a novel generative reward paradigm. By reformulating the reward score as the model’s probability of predicting a "yes" token, indicating that the generated image outperforms a reference image according to specific criteria – RewardDance intrinsically aligns reward objectives with VLM architectures. This alignment unlocks scaling across two dimensions: (1) Model Scaling: Systematic scaling of RMs up to 26 billion parameters; (2) Context Scaling: Integration of task-specific instructions, reference examples, and chain-of-thought (CoT) reasoning. Extensive experiments demonstrate that RewardDance significantly surpasses state-of-the-art methods in text-to-image, text-to-video, and image-to-video generation. Crucially, we resolve the persistent challenge of "reward hacking": Our large-scale RMs exhibit and maintain high reward variance during RL fine-tuning, proving their resistance to hacking and ability to produce diverse, high-quality outputs. It greatly relieves the mode collapse problem that plagues smaller models.
items.zh: 奖励模型（RM）对于通过强化学习（RL）改进生成模型至关重要，然而视觉生成中的RM缩放范式在很大程度上仍未被探索。这主要源于现有方法的基本局限性：基于CLIP的RM受限于架构和输入模态，而广泛使用的Bradley-Terry损失与视觉语言模型（VLM）的下一个词预测机制根本不对齐，阻碍了有效缩放。更关键的是，RLHF优化过程受到奖励破解问题的困扰，模型利用奖励信号的缺陷而不改进真实质量。为解决这些挑战，我们引入了RewardDance，一个可扩展的奖励建模框架，通过一种新颖的生成式奖励范式克服了这些障碍。通过将奖励分数重新定义为模型预测“是”词元的概率，表示生成的图像根据特定标准优于参考图像——RewardDance内在地将奖励目标与VLM架构对齐。这种对齐解锁了两个维度的缩放：（1）模型缩放：将RM系统性地扩展到260亿参数；（2）上下文缩放：整合任务特定指令、参考示例和思维链（CoT）推理。大量实验表明，RewardDance在文本到图像、文本到视频和图像到视频生成中显著超越了最先进的方法。关键的是，我们解决了“奖励破解”这一持久挑战：我们的大规模RM在RL微调期间表现出并保持高奖励方差，证明了其抵抗破解的能力以及产生多样化、高质量输出的能力。这极大地缓解了困扰较小模型的模式崩溃问题。

items.capEn: Figure 1: RewardDance consistently boosts T2I and T2V generation (validated by alignment/GSB). The reward variance during the later stages of RL training, represented by bubble size, serves as an indicator of policy "hacking." Low variance implies mode collapse, where the model tends to generate uniform, high-reward outputs. High variance signifies the policy maintains output diversity across various prompts, indicating that it has not collapsed.
items.capZh: 图1：RewardDance持续提升T2I和T2V生成（通过对齐/GSB验证）。RL训练后期阶段的奖励方差，由气泡大小表示，作为策略“破解”的指标。低方差意味着模式崩溃，模型倾向于生成统一的高奖励输出。高方差表示策略在各种提示下保持输出多样性，表明其未崩溃。

items.en: Diffusion models have achieved immense progress in visual generation. State-of-the-art models such as FLUX [26] and Seedream [16, 14] for image generation, alongside Wan2.1 [56] and Seedance [15] for video generation, have unlocked vast creative potential. These capabilities are further enhanced by paradigms like Reinforcement Learning (RL) [55, 11, 13, 20, 67, 34, 28, 29, 18] and Test-time Scaling [39], where the Reward Model (RM) plays a pivotal role. While a robust and accurate RM can significantly improve generation quality, the community has lacked clear guidance on designing superior RMs. In this paper, we introduce RewardDance, a new framework built on the principle that scalability is the key to creating better visual RMs.
items.zh: 扩散模型在视觉生成方面取得了巨大进展。最先进的模型如用于图像生成的FLUX [26]和Seedream [16, 14]，以及用于视频生成的Wan2.1 [56]和Seedance [15]，释放了巨大的创造潜力。这些能力通过强化学习（RL）[55, 11, 13, 20, 67, 34, 28, 29, 18]和测试时缩放[39]等范式进一步增强，其中奖励模型（RM）起着关键作用。虽然一个稳健且准确的RM可以显著提高生成质量，但社区缺乏关于如何设计更优RM的明确指导。在本文中，我们介绍了RewardDance，一个基于可扩展性是创建更好视觉RM的关键这一原则的新框架。

items.en: Our work is motivated by a comprehensive analysis of existing reward modeling methods, as summarized in Table 1. Early CLIP-based reward models [64, 63] were limited by CLIP’s architecture, which was difficult to scale [31] and generalized poorly to diverse tasks [30]. Later VLM-based models explored new paradigms and scaling strategies, but progress has been fragmented: some achieve large-scale models yet remain limited to regressive paradigms (e.g., HPSv3 [40] , WorldPM [57]), while others adopt stronger generative paradigms without effective scaling (e.g., UnifiedReward [59]). As shown in the Figure 2 , the regression-based reward model is very susceptible to reward hacking.
items.zh: 我们的工作源于对现有奖励建模方法的全面分析，总结于表1。早期的基于CLIP的奖励模型[64, 63]受限于CLIP的架构，难以缩放[31]且泛化到多样任务的能力差[30]。后来的基于VLM的模型探索了新范式和缩放策略，但进展是零散的：一些实现了大规模模型但仍局限于回归范式（例如，HPSv3 [40]，WorldPM [57]），而另一些采用了更强的生成范式但没有有效缩放（例如，UnifiedReward [59]）。如图2所示，基于回归的奖励模型非常容易受到奖励破解的影响。

items.capEn: Figure 2: Comparison of training dynamics for Regressive vs. Generative reward models during diffusion RL fine-tuning. At the same 2B model scale (Left vs. Middle panel), the generative reward model exhibits significantly superior training dynamics compared to the regression-based one: it facilitates higher exploration magnitude, manifested as greater reward variance, and a more favorable reward growth trend. This higher diversity in reward signals indicates that the generative RM exhibits stronger robustness against reward hacking. Under a regression-based RM, the diffusion model risks learning to exploit reward loopholes to achieve high scores without making substantive progress. This inherent robustness is key to the generative RM’s successful scaling to 26B parameters (Right panel).
items.capZh: 图2：扩散RL微调过程中回归式与生成式奖励模型的训练动态比较。在相同的2B模型规模下（左图与中图），生成式奖励模型表现出显著优于回归式模型的训练动态：它促进了更高的探索幅度，表现为更大的奖励方差，以及更有利的奖励增长趋势。这种更高的奖励信号多样性表明生成式RM对奖励破解具有更强的鲁棒性。在回归式RM下，扩散模型可能学会利用奖励漏洞获得高分而不做出实质性改进。这种固有的鲁棒性是生成式RM成功扩展到26B参数的关键（右图）。

items.en: We introduce RewardDance to address above challenges. It represents the first framework designed to achieve this unification, leveraging a generative paradigm that enables effective scaling across both model size and context—including task-specific instructions, reference examples, and Chain-of-Thought (CoT) reasoning—to unlock the full potential of VLMs for advanced visual reward modeling.
items.zh: 我们引入RewardDance来解决上述挑战。它代表了第一个旨在实现这种统一的框架，利用生成式范式实现模型大小和上下文（包括任务特定指令、参考示例和思维链（CoT）推理）的有效缩放，以释放VLM在高级视觉奖励建模中的全部潜力。

items.en: Our RewardDance framework resolves this fundamental paradigm mismatch by rethinking reward modeling as a generative task. Instead of appending a regression head, we convert the reward score into the VLM’s predicted probability of a "yes" token. This approach natively aligns the reward objective with the VLM’s autoregressive, next-token prediction mechanism, thereby enabling effective reward scaling along two primary dimensions:
items.zh: 我们的RewardDance框架通过将奖励建模重新构想为生成任务来解决这种根本的范式不匹配。我们不添加回归头，而是将奖励分数转换为VLM对“是”词元的预测概率。这种方法原生地将奖励目标与VLM的自回归、下一个词预测机制对齐，从而沿两个主要维度实现有效的奖励缩放：

items.en: Model Scaling: We break from the conventional practice of using smaller, fixed-size models by systematically scaling our RM with VLMs of increasing capacity (1B to 26B parameters). This directly links model parameter count to both reward modeling performance and final generation quality.
items.zh: 模型缩放：我们打破了使用较小的固定大小模型的常规做法，通过系统性地将RM与容量递增的VLM（1B到26B参数）一起缩放。这直接将模型参数数量与奖励建模性能和最终生成质量联系起来。

items.en: Context Scaling: In contrast to traditional methods that rely on simple image-text pairs, RewardDance enriches input context by incorporating task-aware instructions, reference examples, and CoT reasoning data, enabling more robust and accurate reward judgments.
items.zh: 上下文缩放：与依赖简单图像-文本对的传统方法相比，RewardDance通过整合任务感知指令、参考示例和CoT推理数据来丰富输入上下文，从而实现更稳健和准确的奖励判断。

items.en: To validate RewardDance, we conducted extensive experiments on both open-source and proprietary models across a range of tasks, including text-to-image, text-to-video, and image-to-video generation. We observed that as the parameter of the Reward Model (RM) increases, the diffusion model exhibits stronger exploratory capacity at higher RLHF iteration steps and is less susceptible to reward hacking. Moreover, incorporating additional context information significantly enhances model performance and robustness. Crucially, our findings reveal a pronounced scaling effect: under both RL and test-time scaling paradigms, visual generation quality consistently and stably improves in direct correspondence with reward model enhancement.
items.zh: 为了验证RewardDance，我们在开源和专有模型上进行了大量实验，涵盖文本到图像、文本到视频和图像到视频生成等任务。我们观察到，随着奖励模型（RM）参数的增加，扩散模型在更高的RLHF迭代步骤中表现出更强的探索能力，并且更不容易受到奖励破解的影响。此外，整合额外的上下文信息显著提升了模型性能和鲁棒性。关键的是，我们的发现揭示了显著的缩放效应：在RL和测试时缩放范式下，视觉生成质量随着奖励模型的增强而一致且稳定地提升。

items.en: Scalability as the Principle for RMs: We establish scalability as the fundamental design principle for visual RMs, addressing a critical yet underexplored dimension in existing work and providing new insights for the field. More importantly, our experiments demonstrate that scaling up the Reward Model (RM) enables less reward hacking (Fig. 2) and further achieves significant improvements in generation quality (Fig. 1).
items.zh: 可扩展性作为RM的原则：我们将可扩展性确立为视觉RM的基本设计原则，解决了现有工作中一个关键但未被充分探索的维度，并为该领域提供了新的见解。更重要的是，我们的实验表明，扩大奖励模型（RM）可以减少奖励破解（图2），并进一步实现生成质量的显著提升（图1）。

items.en: Generative Reward Modeling: We propose a novel generative reward modeling paradigm that resolves the fundamental mismatch in prior work by regarding reward prediction as a next-token prediction task. This naturally aligns with VLMs’ autogressive mechanism, unlocking the potential for effective reward scaling.
items.zh: 生成式奖励建模：我们提出了一种新颖的生成式奖励建模范式，通过将奖励预测视为下一个词预测任务，解决了先前工作中的根本不匹配。这自然地与VLM的自回归机制对齐，释放了有效奖励缩放的潜力。

items.en: Comprehensive Reward Scaling: We propose and validate a holistic methodology for scaling RMs across two dimensions: reward model size (from 1B to 26B parameters) and reward context (including task-aware instructions, reference examples, and CoT reasoning). We are the first to systematically demonstrate that enhancing these dimensions yields stable and consistent scaling effects, where improved reward models directly contribute to higher-quality visual generation.
items.zh: 全面的奖励缩放：我们提出并验证了一种全面的方法论，用于在两个维度上缩放RM：奖励模型大小（从1B到26B参数）和奖励上下文（包括任务感知指令、参考示例和CoT推理）。我们是第一个系统性地证明增强这些维度能产生稳定且一致的缩放效应，其中改进的奖励模型直接贡献于更高质量的视觉生成。

items.en: Generalized diffusion models encompass both traditional diffusion processes [52, 21, 53] and flow-based models [33]. These methods learn to map a simple prior distribution, typically a Gaussian, to a target data distribution by reversing a learned noise-injection process. Owing to their effectiveness in modeling complex, high-dimensional data, diffusion models have demonstrated remarkable performance across a wide range of tasks, including image [49, 44, 12, 16, 26, 14, 32, 71] and video [4, 19, 6, 1, 15, 51, 69, 25, 56, 38, 70, 8] generation.
items.zh: 广义扩散模型包括传统的扩散过程[52, 21, 53]和基于流的模型[33]。这些方法通过逆转一个学习到的噪声注入过程，学习将简单的先验分布（通常是高斯分布）映射到目标数据分布。由于其在建模复杂高维数据方面的有效性，扩散模型在广泛的任务中表现出色，包括图像[49, 44, 12, 16, 26, 14, 32, 71]和视频[4, 19, 6, 1, 15, 51, 69, 25, 56, 38, 70, 8]生成。

items.en: Reward Models (RMs) play a pivotal role in aligning the outputs of visual generation models with human preferences [48, 71, 15, 16, 14]. Early approaches, such as ImageReward [64], PickScore [23], and HPSv2 [63], fine-tuned CLIP models to produce scores that reflect human preferences. Subsequent works [65, 35] adopted Vision-Language Models (VLMs) as backbones, typically appending a regression head to output reward signals. These methods, predominantly trained with the Bradley–Terry loss [5, 43, 72], are categorized as regression-based RMs. More recently, advancements in LLMs and multimodal learning have inspired new paradigms. For instance, WorldPM [57] investigated the scaling potential of RMs through enhanced data and model architectures. Concurrently, a new class of generative reward models was proposed [36, 66, 59], aiming to better leverage the native capabilities of pre-trained models. Building upon these developments, our work introduces the generative RM paradigm to the visual domain and, for the first time, systematically explores its scaling properties with respect to both reward model size and reward context.
items.zh: 奖励模型（RM）在将视觉生成模型的输出与人类偏好对齐方面起着关键作用[48, 71, 15, 16, 14]。早期方法，如ImageReward [64]、PickScore [23]和HPSv2 [63]，微调CLIP模型以产生反映人类偏好的分数。后续工作[65, 35]采用视觉语言模型（VLM）作为骨干，通常附加一个回归头来输出奖励信号。这些方法主要使用Bradley-Terry损失[5, 43, 72]训练，被归类为基于回归的RM。最近，LLM和多模态学习的进步激发了新的范式。例如，WorldPM [57]通过增强的数据和模型架构研究了RM的缩放潜力。同时，提出了一类新的生成式奖励模型[36, 66, 59]，旨在更好地利用预训练模型的原生能力。在这些发展的基础上，我们的工作将生成式RM范式引入视觉领域，并首次系统地探索了其在奖励模型大小和奖励上下文方面的缩放特性。

full.secZh: 2.3 基于人类反馈的强化学习

items.en: Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF) has been increasingly adopted in diffusion models to align generated outputs with human preferences. The typical pipeline involves training a reward model on human preference data and leveraging it to guide the generative model. For instance, DDPO [3] adapts Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO) to diffusion frameworks by computing image log-likelihoods. In contrast, ReFL [64] circumvents likelihood computation challenges by directly optimizing diffusion outputs through gradients from a frozen reward model. Beyond diffusion models, recent works [68, 67, 34, 35] have extended RLHF techniques to flow-based generative models, demonstrating the broader applicability of preference-based optimization in generative modeling. Complementing training-time alignment methods, test-time optimization techniques have emerged to enhance diffusion model performance during inference without requiring model retraining. These approaches [39, 42, 35] typically involve reward-guided adjustments to noise prediction outputs or dynamic modifications to sampling schedules, thereby improving generation quality in a plug-and-play manner. In this work, we integrate our RewardDance framework into both reinforcement learning and inference-time optimization stages, demonstrating its effectiveness in aligning generated content with human preferences across diverse visual generation tasks.
items.zh: 基于人类反馈的强化学习（RLHF）越来越多地被用于扩散模型，以将生成的输出与人类偏好对齐。典型的流程包括在人类偏好数据上训练奖励模型，并利用它来指导生成模型。例如，DDPO [3]通过计算图像对数似然将近端策略优化（PPO）适应到扩散框架。相比之下，ReFL [64]通过直接通过冻结奖励模型的梯度优化扩散输出来规避似然计算的挑战。除了扩散模型，最近的工作[68, 67, 34, 35]将RLHF技术扩展到基于流的生成模型，展示了基于偏好的优化在生成建模中的更广泛适用性。作为训练时对齐方法的补充，测试时优化技术出现，用于在推理期间增强扩散模型性能而无需重新训练模型。这些方法[39, 42, 35]通常涉及奖励引导的噪声预测输出调整或采样计划的动态修改，从而以即插即用的方式提高生成质量。在这项工作中，我们将我们的RewardDance框架整合到强化学习和推理时优化阶段，展示了其在各种视觉生成任务中将生成内容与人类偏好对齐的有效性。

items.en: This section introduces RewardDance, our framework for scalable visual reward modeling. We first detail its core generative paradigm, then describe our methodologies for scaling it along two primary dimensions: context and model size. Finally, we outline the training pipeline in aligning diffusion models.
items.zh: 本节介绍RewardDance，我们用于可扩展视觉奖励建模的框架。我们首先详细说明其核心生成范式，然后描述我们沿两个主要维度（上下文和模型大小）进行缩放的方法。最后，我们概述了对齐扩散模型的训练流程。

items.en: As illustrated in Figure 3 (top panel), conventional reward modeling approaches follow a pointwise regressive paradigm. The term "pointwise" indicates that these RMs evaluate individual images independently, while "regressive" refers to the scalar reward prediction mechanism. This paradigm encompasses both CLIP-based models, which compute rewards through cosine similarity between CLIP image and text embeddings, and VLM-based approaches, which typically employ additional regression heads to map VLM hidden states to scalar reward values. These models are commonly optimized using preference pairs with the Bradley-Terry (BT) loss:
items.zh: 如图3（上图）所示，传统的奖励建模方法遵循逐点回归范式。术语“逐点”表示这些RM独立评估单个图像，而“回归”指的是标量奖励预测机制。该范式包括基于CLIP的模型（通过CLIP图像和文本嵌入之间的余弦相似度计算奖励）和基于VLM的方法（通常使用额外的回归头将VLM隐藏状态映射到标量奖励值）。这些模型通常使用偏好对和Bradley-Terry（BT）损失进行优化：

items.en: where yy is the input prompt, xwx^{w} and xlx^{l} are the chosen (better) and rejected (worse) images, r​(⋅,⋅)r(\cdot,\cdot) is the reward model, and σ​(⋅)\sigma(\cdot) is the sigmoid function. These approaches ultimately provide reward signals to optimize diffusion models through reward-based feedback learning.
items.zh: 其中yy是输入提示，xwx^{w}和xlx^{l}是选择（更好）和拒绝（更差）的图像，r​(⋅,⋅)r(\cdot,\cdot)是奖励模型，σ​(⋅)\sigma(\cdot)是sigmoid函数。这些方法最终通过基于奖励的反馈学习为优化扩散模型提供奖励信号。

items.en: However, this regressive paradigm suffers from fundamental limitations. CLIP-based approaches are constrained by their dual-encoder architecture and single-modality design, which impose inherent scalability bottlenecks. For VLM-based models, the regression head introduces a critical paradigm mismatch with the model’s native next-token prediction ability. Collectively, these limitations prevent full utilization of pre-trained knowledge and fundamentally restrict effective scaling across both reward context and model size dimensions.
items.zh: 然而，这种回归范式存在根本性局限。基于CLIP的方法受限于其双编码器架构和单模态设计，这带来了固有的可扩展性瓶颈。对于基于VLM的模型，回归头引入了与模型原生下一个词预测能力的严重范式不匹配。总体而言，这些限制阻碍了预训练知识的充分利用，并从根本上限制了在奖励上下文和模型规模维度上的有效Scaling（规模扩张）。

items.en: To address the limitations of conventional regressive reward modeling, we introduce RewardDance, a novel framework designed for scalable visual reward modeling. Our approach centers on a generative paradigm that regards reward modeling as a token generation task, naturally aligning with the native capabilities of modern VLMs. This core innovation enables systematic exploration of scalability along two primary dimensions: Model Scaling and Context Scaling. As shown in Figure 3 (bottom panel), our RewardDance framework supports models ranging from 1B to 26B parameters. The framework operates through two main stages: (1) RewardDance Training: where we train the reward model on task-aware Chain-of-Thought (CoT) instruction data, and (2) RewardDance Inference: where the trained reward model provides feedback signals to optimize diffusion models through reward feedback learning.
items.zh: 为了解决传统回归奖励建模的局限性，我们引入了RewardDance，一种专为可扩展视觉奖励建模设计的新框架。我们的方法以生成范式为核心，将奖励建模视为词元生成任务，自然对齐现代VLM的原生能力。这一核心创新使得能够沿两个主要维度系统探索可扩展性：模型Scaling（规模扩张）和上下文Scaling（规模扩张）。如图3（底部面板）所示，我们的RewardDance框架支持从1B到26B参数的模型。该框架通过两个主要阶段运行：（1）RewardDance训练：我们在任务感知的思维链（CoT）指令数据上训练奖励模型；（2）RewardDance推理：训练好的奖励模型通过奖励反馈学习提供反馈信号以优化扩散模型。

items.capEn: Figure 3: Overview of RewardDance framework compared with existing reward model Architecture. (Top): Previous works use CLIP-based or VLM-based reward models to provide scalar reward scores for diffusion model training. (Bottom): Our RewardDance approach (from 1B to 26B parameters) uses task-aware CoT instructions for reward modeling with reasoning. Red flames indicate trainable components; blue ice cubes indicate frozen parameters.
items.capZh: 图3：RewardDance框架与现有奖励模型架构的对比概览。（顶部）：先前的工作使用基于CLIP或基于VLM的奖励模型为扩散模型训练提供标量奖励分数。（底部）：我们的RewardDance方法（从1B到26B参数）使用任务感知的CoT指令进行带推理的奖励建模。红色火焰表示可训练组件；蓝色冰块表示冻结参数。

items.en: We scale the context of our reward model beyond simple image-prompt pairs by incorporating three key elements: task-aware instructions, reference images, and Chain-of-Thought (CoT) reasoning.
items.zh: 我们将奖励模型的上下文扩展到简单的图像-提示对之外，通过纳入三个关键元素：任务感知指令、参考图像和思维链（CoT）推理。

items.en: First, we regard the reward scoring problem as a comparative judgment task as shown in Figure 3 (bottom left). The model’s input consists of image1 tokens x1x_{1}, image2 tokens x2x_{2}, prompt yy, and CoT task instruction ii. The task instruction guides the model to evaluate whether one image is superior to the other based on a set of predefined criteria. The model is then trained to predict the next token with "yes" or "no". In practice, the reward score is the predicted probability of the "yes" token:
items.zh: 首先，我们将奖励评分问题视为一个比较判断任务，如图3（左下）所示。模型的输入由图像1词元x1、图像2词元x2、提示y和CoT任务指令i组成。任务指令引导模型根据一组预定义标准评估一幅图像是否优于另一幅。然后训练模型预测下一个词元为“是”或“否”。在实践中，奖励分数是“是”词元的预测概率：

items.en: where PθP_{\theta} is the probability distribution from our VLM-based reward model, RewardDance.
items.zh: 其中Pθ是我们基于VLM的奖励模型RewardDance的概率分布。

items.en: To further scale the context and enhance the RM’s reasoning capabilities, we train it to generate a rationale for its decision in a Chain-of-Thought (CoT) manner. As illustrated in the Figure 3, the model outputs both "yes/no" and "Reasons" tokens. We construct two data formats for this: one where the model outputs the "yes/no" decision before the reasoning, and another where it generates the reasoning first and concludes with the decision. This detailed reasoning data, distilled from a powerful teacher model (SEED-VL 1.5), not only improves performance but also makes the reward judgments interpretable. For efficiency during the feedback alignment stage (bottom right in the Figure 3), we employ the first format and derive the reward signal by maximizing the probability of the "yes" token: P​(yes|…)P(\text{yes}|...) to optimize the diffusion model.
items.zh: 为了进一步扩展上下文并增强奖励模型的推理能力，我们训练它以思维链（CoT）方式生成其决策的理由。如图3所示，模型输出“是/否”和“理由”词元。我们为此构建了两种数据格式：一种模型在推理前输出“是/否”决策，另一种先生成推理再以决策结束。这些从强大教师模型（SEED-VL 1.5）中蒸馏出的详细推理数据不仅提高了性能，还使奖励判断具有可解释性。为了在反馈对齐阶段（图3右下）提高效率，我们采用第一种格式，并通过最大化“是”词元的概率P(yes|…)来导出奖励信号，以优化扩散模型。

items.capEn: Figure 4: Examples of task-aware instruction and CoT response used for training our RewardDance model.
items.capZh: 图4：用于训练我们的RewardDance模型的任务感知指令和CoT响应示例。

items.en: To investigate the effects of model scale, we systematically scale our reward model using variants of the InternVL [10] architecture, ranging from 1B to 26B parameters. Our experiments reveal a strong positive correlation between the reward model’s parameter count and the final quality of generated outputs, demonstrating that larger reward models yield superior reward evaluation performance and enhanced generation quality.
items.zh: 为了研究模型规模的影响，我们使用InternVL [10]架构的变体系统扩展我们的奖励模型，范围从1B到26B参数。我们的实验揭示了奖励模型参数数量与最终生成输出质量之间的强正相关性，表明更大的奖励模型能产生更优的奖励评估性能和增强的生成质量。

items.en: Pointwise Generative Variant: While our primary reference (pairwise) generative reward paradigm requires reference images for reward prediction, we also design a pointwise generative variant. This variant receives only prompts and generated images, incorporating additional instructions typically used to evaluate whether generated results meet high-quality standards. The reward value is measured by the probability of the "yes" token. During training, we employ Bradley-Terry loss for preference pairs. To facilitate better model convergence, we add a weighted cross-entropy (CE) loss with a small coefficient, assigning "yes" labels to preferred samples and "no" labels to rejected samples.
items.zh: 逐点生成变体：虽然我们的主要参考（成对）生成奖励范式需要参考图像进行奖励预测，但我们也设计了一种逐点生成变体。该变体仅接收提示和生成图像，并纳入通常用于评估生成结果是否满足高质量标准的额外指令。奖励值通过“是”词元的概率衡量。在训练期间，我们对偏好对采用Bradley-Terry损失。为了促进更好的模型收敛，我们添加了一个带小系数的加权交叉熵（CE）损失，为偏好样本分配“是”标签，为拒绝样本分配“否”标签。

items.en: For Reinforcement Learning (RL)-based fine-tuning, we adopt the ReFL algorithm [64]. Our generative reward model, RewardDance, provides crucial preference signals. Since our model operates comparatively, we utilize a Best-of-N (BoN) sampling strategy to establish high-quality references for each prompt. Specifically, we first generate N candidate images using the SFT model. RewardDance then performs pairwise comparisons among candidates to identify the top-ranked image, which serves as a reference in subsequent fine-tuning steps. We explore different strategies for selecting reference images based on win counts, with detailed experiments presented in Section 4.4.
items.zh: 对于基于强化学习（RL）的微调，我们采用ReFL算法[64]。我们的生成式奖励模型RewardDance提供了关键的偏好信号。由于我们的模型以比较方式运行，我们利用Best-of-N（BoN）采样策略为每个提示建立高质量参考。具体来说，我们首先使用SFT模型生成N个候选图像。然后RewardDance在候选图像之间进行成对比较，以识别排名最高的图像，该图像在后续微调步骤中作为参考。我们探索了基于获胜次数选择参考图像的不同策略，详细实验见第4.4节。

items.en: To further enhance inference-time performance, we implement a Search over Paths approach [36]. This strategy works by pruning a search space of multiple generation paths to select the most promising trajectories. The process begins by initializing N distinct sampling trajectories from different noise vectors. During ODE sampling, these trajectories are iteratively extended using a re-noising and re-sampling mechanism. Specifically, a verifier (our RewardDance) prunes the search space at each step by selecting the most promising trajectories. For this verification role, we deploy a computationally efficient point-wisee generative variant of our model. This lightweight variant is optimized for speed by omitting reference images and evaluating individual candidate images based solely on task instructions.
items.zh: 为了进一步增强推理时性能，我们实现了一种路径搜索方法[36]。该策略通过剪枝多个生成路径的搜索空间来选择最有希望的轨迹。该过程首先从不同的噪声向量初始化N个不同的采样轨迹。在ODE采样期间，这些轨迹通过重新噪声化和重新采样机制迭代扩展。具体来说，验证器（我们的RewardDance）通过选择最有希望的轨迹在每一步剪枝搜索空间。对于此验证角色，我们部署了一个计算高效的逐点生成变体模型。该轻量级变体通过省略参考图像并仅基于任务指令评估单个候选图像来优化速度。

items.en: In this section, we present a comprehensive evaluation of RewardDance on both text-to-image, text-to-video and image-to-video tasks. We analyze the impact of reward model scaling, inference-time scaling and various architectural choices through detailed experimental results and ablation studies.
items.zh: 在本节中，我们在文本到图像、文本到视频和图像到视频任务上对RewardDance进行全面评估。我们通过详细的实验结果和消融研究分析了奖励模型Scaling（规模扩张）、推理时Scaling（规模扩张）以及各种架构选择的影响。

items.en: Image Baselines. Our primary baselines include representative state-of-the-art (SOTA) models from academia (e.g., LlamaGen [54], SD-v1.51.5 [49], PixArt-α\alpha [7], SD-v2.12.1 [49], DALL-E 22 [45], Emu33-Gen [58], SDXL [44], DALL-E 33 [2], SD-v3 [12] and FLUX.1-dev [27]) and industry (e.g., Luma [37], Ideogram [22], Midjourney V6.1 [41], DALLE-3 [2], FLUX1.1 [27], Hunyuan [25] , Imagen 3, Recraft [47]s, Seedream 2.0 [16] and Qwen-Image [61]).
items.zh: 图像基线。我们的主要基线包括来自学术界（例如，LlamaGen [54]、SD-v1.5 [49]、PixArt-α [7]、SD-v2.1 [49]、DALL-E 2 [45]、Emu3-Gen [58]、SDXL [44]、DALL-E 3 [2]、SD-v3 [12]和FLUX.1-dev [27]）和工业界（例如，Luma [37]、Ideogram [22]、Midjourney V6.1 [41]、DALLE-3 [2]、FLUX1.1 [27]、Hunyuan [25]、Imagen 3、Recraft [47]、Seedream 2.0 [16]和Qwen-Image [61]）的代表性最先进（SOTA）模型。

items.en: Video Baselines. We compare our model with SOTA industry products, e.g., Kling 2.1 Master [24], Veo-3.0 [17], Kling 2.0 Master [24], Veo-2.0 [17], Wan 2.1 [56], Sora [6], RunwayGen 4 [50].
items.zh: 视频基线。我们将我们的模型与SOTA工业产品进行比较，例如Kling 2.1 Master [24]、Veo-3.0 [17]、Kling 2.0 Master [24]、Veo-2.0 [17]、Wan 2.1 [56]、Sora [6]、RunwayGen 4 [50]。

items.en: Image Evaluation Benchmarks. We adopt Bench-240, the evaluation prompts utilized in Seedream 2.0 and Seedream 3.0. This comprehensive set of 240 prompts is constructed by integrating representative prompts from publicly available benchmarks with manually curated examples. The prompt distribution is rigorously calibrated based on extensive user preference surveys. The maximum score for Bench-240 is 100 points.
items.zh: 图像评估基准。我们采用Bench-240，即Seedream 2.0和Seedream 3.0中使用的评估提示。这组包含240个提示的综合集合通过将公开基准中的代表性提示与手动整理的示例相结合构建而成。提示分布基于广泛的用户偏好调查进行严格校准。Bench-240的最高得分为100分。

items.en: Video Evaluation Benchmarks. For a comprehensive evaluation of video generation models across diverse scenarios, we utilize SeedVideoBench-1.0, introduced in Seedance 1.0. It comprises 300 prompts applicable to both text-to-video and image-to-video tasks, covering a wide spectrum of application domains. The taxonomy for the image-to-video task aligns with that of text-to-video, augmented with detailed annotations for the initial frame.
items.zh: 视频评估基准。为了全面评估不同场景下的视频生成模型，我们利用Seedance 1.0中引入的SeedVideoBench-1.0。它包含300个提示，适用于文本到视频和图像到视频任务，覆盖广泛的应用领域。图像到视频任务的分类法与文本到视频一致，并增加了初始帧的详细注释。

items.en: Image Metrics. For all text-to-image experiments, we adopt the image-text alignment score from [14]. This metric involves human evaluators to verify whether the generated images correctly depict the key elements described in the prompts.
items.zh: 图像指标。对于所有文本到图像实验，我们采用来自[14]的图像-文本对齐分数。该指标涉及人类评估者，以验证生成的图像是否正确描述了提示中描述的关键元素。

items.en: Video Metrics. For text-to-video and image-to-video evaluation, we use the human preference-based Good-Same-Bad (GSB) score. Here, G, S, and B represent the counts of "Good," "Same," and "Bad" judgments, respectively. The final GSB score is defined as:
items.zh: 视频指标。对于文本到视频和图像到视频评估，我们使用基于人类偏好的Good-Same-Bad（GSB）分数。这里，G、S和B分别表示“好”、“相同”和“差”判断的计数。最终GSB分数定义为：

items.en: To enable performance comparison with industry products, we developed the Video-Text Alignment Score. The scoring rubric is defined as follows: 0 indicates a complete mismatch between the video content and the text description; 1 indicates a partial match; and 2 indicates a perfect match.
items.zh: 为了能够与工业产品进行性能比较，我们开发了视频-文本对齐分数。评分规则定义如下：0表示视频内容与文本描述完全不匹配；1表示部分匹配；2表示完美匹配。

full.secZh: 4.2 与奖励模型规模化的比较

items.en: Image Generation. As demonstrated in Table 2, deploying and scaling RewardDance delivers substantial performance improvements across both RL fine-tuning and test-time scaling paradigms. For RL fine-tuning, scaling the RM from 1B to 26B parameters significantly enhances FLUX.1-dev performance from 67.0 to 73.6. This scaling effect becomes even more pronounced with Seedream-3.0, where scores dramatically increase from a baseline of 74.1 to an impressive 84.8 using the 26B RM. A consistent scaling trend emerges for test-time scaling with Seedream-3.0, with performance steadily climbing from 74.1 to 80.5. This robust correlation across diverse base models and optimization paradigms confirms that larger VLM-based reward models more effectively capture human preferences, thereby guiding diffusion models toward superior output quality.
items.zh: 图像生成。如表2所示，部署和扩展RewardDance在强化学习微调和测试时扩展两种范式下均带来了显著的性能提升。对于强化学习微调，将奖励模型从1B参数扩展到26B参数，使FLUX.1-dev的性能从67.0显著提升至73.6。这种扩展效应在Seedream-3.0上更为明显，使用26B奖励模型时，得分从基线74.1大幅提升至令人印象深刻的84.8。对于Seedream-3.0的测试时扩展，也出现了一致的扩展趋势，性能从74.1稳步提升至80.5。这种跨不同基础模型和优化范式的稳健相关性证实，基于更大视觉语言模型的奖励模型能更有效地捕捉人类偏好，从而引导扩散模型生成更高质量的输出。

items.en: Furthermore, we constructed two evaluation datasets: In-Domain (ID) preference dataset contains 2,500 sample pairs. This dataset was constructed by partitioning a subset of data points from the Stage 2 training set of RewardDance that were held out and unseen during training. Out-Of-Domain (OOD) preference dataset contains over 4,000 sample pairs. This dataset was curated by sampling from public benchmark datasets, including ImageReward [64] and HPS [62], to assess the model’s generalization capability on out-of-distribution samples.
items.zh: 此外，我们构建了两个评估数据集：领域内偏好数据集包含2500个样本对，通过从RewardDance第二阶段训练集中划分出一部分在训练中未见的保留数据点构建而成；领域外偏好数据集包含超过4000个样本对，通过从ImageReward [64]和HPS [62]等公开基准数据集中采样构建，以评估模型在分布外样本上的泛化能力。

items.en: As shown in Table 2, we observe no strict positive correlation between the Reward Model (RM) accuracy on the ID dataset and its parameter scale, particularly evident in models with 1B, 2B, and 4B parameters. This finding aligns with existing studies [9, 46, 60], which suggest that higher RM accuracy does not guarantee corresponding improvements in Reinforcement Learning (RL) performance. However, the RM’s accuracy on the OOD dataset – indicative of its generalization capability – can be viewed as a more significant predictor of the final outcome. An RM that demonstrates accurate judgment on unseen distributions and strong generalization capability possesses substantially higher guiding value. Consequently, for evaluating RM efficacy, OOD accuracy (generalization capability) emerges as a more critical core metric than ID accuracy. Building upon this insight, developing evaluation benchmarks that better assess RM generalization constitutes an important direction for future research. This involves enhancing the consistency between the RM evaluation set and the RL test set, as well as integrating sample pairs of varying difficulty levels to enable a more comprehensive assessment of reward model capabilities.
items.zh: 如表2所示，我们观察到奖励模型在领域内数据集上的准确率与其参数规模之间并不存在严格的正相关关系，这在1B、2B和4B参数的模型中尤为明显。这一发现与现有研究[9, 46, 60]一致，表明更高的奖励模型准确率并不能保证强化学习性能的相应提升。然而，奖励模型在领域外数据集上的准确率——反映其泛化能力——可被视为最终结果的更重要预测指标。一个能在未见分布上做出准确判断并具备强泛化能力的奖励模型，具有更高的指导价值。因此，在评估奖励模型有效性时，领域外准确率（泛化能力）比领域内准确率更关键。基于这一见解，开发能更好评估奖励模型泛化能力的评估基准是未来研究的重要方向，这包括增强奖励模型评估集与强化学习测试集之间的一致性，以及整合不同难度级别的样本对，以实现对奖励模型能力的更全面评估。

items.en: Video Generation. We demonstrate that the benefits of reward scaling extend seamlessly to video generation tasks, as presented in Table 3. We employ GSB metric for this evaluation. For Seedance-1.0 in the Text-to-Video (T2V) RL setting, RewardDance exhibits a compelling scaling trend: performance improves by +28% with a 1B RM compared to Seedance-1.0-SFT, and progressively increases to an impressive +49% improvement when utilizing the 26B RM. This scaling phenomenon is equally pronounced in the Image-to-Video (I2V) paradigm, where performance gains systematically increase from +29% with the 1B RM to a substantial +47% with the 26B RM. These consistent improvements across both video generation modalities demonstrate the broad applicability and robustness of our RewardDance framework.
items.zh: 视频生成。我们证明奖励扩展的益处无缝延伸至视频生成任务，如表3所示。我们采用GSB指标进行评估。对于文本到视频强化学习设置下的Seedance-1.0，RewardDance展现出令人信服的扩展趋势：与Seedance-1.0-SFT相比，使用1B奖励模型时性能提升+28%，使用26B奖励模型时性能逐步提升至令人印象深刻的+49%。这种扩展现象在图像到视频范式中同样显著，性能增益从使用1B奖励模型时的+29%系统性地增长到使用26B奖励模型时的+47%。这两种视频生成模态的一致改进证明了我们RewardDance框架的广泛适用性和稳健性。

full.secZh: 4.3 与最先进生成模型的比较

items.en: To rigorously assess the competitiveness of our framework, we benchmark our RewardDance-optimized models against leading academic and industrial systems on three benchmarks: GenEval, Bench-240, and SeedVideoBench-1.0. As shown in Tables 4, 5, and 6, models enhanced by RewardDance consistently achieve state-of-the-art performance.
items.zh: 为了严格评估我们框架的竞争力，我们在三个基准（GenEval、Bench-240和SeedVideoBench-1.0）上，将我们的RewardDance优化模型与领先的学术和工业系统进行对比。如表4、5和6所示，经RewardDance增强的模型持续取得最先进的性能。

items.en: GenEval. As shown in Table 4, models enhanced with RewardDance demonstrate significant superiority across multiple dimensions of text-to-image generation. Seedream-3.0 w RewardDance achieves the top overall score of 0.79, while FLUX.1-dev w RewardDance follows closely at 0.75, outperforming strong baselines like SD3 (0.74). RewardDance confers overall performance gains of +0.10 and +0.09 on Seedream-3.0 w/o RewardDance and FLUX.1-dev, respectively. These advancements validate the efficacy of RewardDance in substantially enhancing semantic understanding and generative precision.
items.zh: GenEval。如表4所示，经RewardDance增强的模型在文本到图像生成的多个维度上展现出显著优势。Seedream-3.0 w RewardDance以0.79的总分位居榜首，FLUX.1-dev w RewardDance以0.75紧随其后，优于SD3（0.74）等强基线。RewardDance在Seedream-3.0 w/o RewardDance和FLUX.1-dev上分别带来了+0.10和+0.09的整体性能提升。这些进展验证了RewardDance在显著增强语义理解和生成精度方面的有效性。

items.en: Bench-240. On the Bench-240 benchmark (Table 5), Seedream-3.0 w RewardDance achieves the highest Overall Score of 0.848 when compared against a suite of powerful, closed-source models. It surpasses top commercial offerings such as Imagen 3 (0.79), Luma (0.77), and Midjourney V6.1 (0.63). A detailed breakdown reveals that our model demonstrates exceptional performance in complex categories like Action (0.87) and is highly competitive in Attribute understanding (0.89), validating the effectiveness of our training and alignment methodology.
items.zh: Bench-240。在Bench-240基准上（表5），Seedream-3.0 w RewardDance与一系列强大的闭源模型相比，取得了最高的总体得分0.848。它超越了Imagen 3（0.79）、Luma（0.77）和Midjourney V6.1（0.63）等顶级商业产品。详细分解显示，我们的模型在动作（0.87）等复杂类别中表现出色，在属性理解（0.89）方面极具竞争力，验证了我们的训练和对齐方法的有效性。

items.en: SeedVideoBench-1.0. We also benchmark our method with Video-Text Alignment Score on video generation tasks (Table 6), evaluating our RewardDance-aligned Seedance 1.0 against leading proprietary models. The final score is the average rating calculated over all samples. In the Text-to-Video (T2V) evaluation, Seedance 1.0 achieves the highest average score of 1.66, outperforming strong competitors like Veo-3.0 (1.63) and Kling 2.1 (1.57). For the Image-to-Video (I2V) task, our model is also state-of-the-art, achieving a top-tier score of 1.65 and tying with the best-performing model, Kling 2.1. These findings underscore our framework’s ability to produce highly competitive, state-of-the-art video generations.
items.zh: SeedVideoBench-1.0。我们还在视频生成任务上使用视频-文本对齐分数对我们的方法进行基准测试（表6），将我们的RewardDance对齐的Seedance 1.0与领先的专有模型进行比较。最终分数是所有样本的平均评分。在文本到视频评估中，Seedance 1.0以1.66的最高平均分胜出，优于Veo-3.0（1.63）和Kling 2.1（1.57）等强劲竞争对手。对于图像到视频任务，我们的模型也达到了最先进水平，取得了1.65的顶级分数，与表现最佳的模型Kling 2.1持平。这些发现强调了我们的框架能够生成极具竞争力的、最先进的视频生成结果。

items.en: Reward Dynamics Analysis. Figure 5 and Figure 6 (first five line graphs) illustrates the reward curves during ReFL training for reward models of varying scales across image and video tasks. In these visualizations, solid lines represent smoothed reward trajectories, and the surrounding light-colored bands capture the actual fluctuation ranges of raw reward values. The bubbles quantify reward variance magnitude within a sliding window of 1,000 RL iteration, simultaneously reflecting the exploration intensity of the current policy.
items.zh: 奖励动态分析。图5和图6（前五个折线图）展示了在图像和视频任务中，不同规模奖励模型在ReFL训练期间的奖励曲线。在这些可视化中，实线表示平滑后的奖励轨迹，周围的浅色带捕捉了原始奖励值的实际波动范围。气泡量化了在1000次强化学习迭代的滑动窗口内的奖励方差大小，同时反映了当前策略的探索强度。

items.capEn: Figure 5: This figure shows the reward curves for Seedream during the RL stage, experiments with a separate reward model of varying sizes (1B to 26B). While reward scores consistently improve with more RL iterations across all models, a key trade-off emerges with RM size: larger RMs tend to exhibit a higher standard deviation, suggesting stronger robustness and less susceptibility to reward hacking.
items.capZh: 图5：该图展示了Seedream在强化学习阶段使用不同规模（1B至26B）独立奖励模型的奖励曲线。虽然所有模型的奖励分数都随着强化学习迭代次数的增加而持续提高，但奖励模型规模带来一个关键权衡：较大的奖励模型往往表现出更高的标准差，表明其稳健性更强，更不易受到奖励破解的影响。

items.capEn: Figure 6: This figure shows the GSB alignment scores for Seedance during the RL stage, experiments with a separate reward model of varying sizes (1B to 26B). Note that the apparent sparsity of fluctuations in this plot (compared to the image task results in Figure 5) stems from the lower sampling frequency of data points recorded for the video task. While reward scores consistently improve with more RL iterations, which phenomenon is consistent with Seedream.
items.capZh: 图6：该图展示了Seedance在强化学习阶段使用不同规模（1B至26B）独立奖励模型的GSB对齐分数。注意，该图中波动的明显稀疏性（与图5中的图像任务结果相比）源于视频任务记录数据点的采样频率较低。虽然奖励分数随着强化学习迭代次数的增加而持续提高，这一现象与Seedream一致。

items.en: Key findings from these analyses include: i) Sustained Exploration in Large-Scale Reward Models: The 26B RM exhibits substantially greater variance throughout the training. Critically, even at advanced training iterations, these error bars remain considerably wide, indicating that the model maintains significant exploration capabilities well into the later training phases. ii) Accelerated Convergence in Smaller Models: In contrast, error bars for 2B/1B RMs converge to narrow ranges much earlier in training (virtually disappearing in later stages), signifying that their policies achieve stable convergence states sooner and cease effective exploration. iii) Positive Scaling-Exploration Correlation: Consistently, larger model scales correspond to wider error bars across all experimental conditions. This strongly suggests that large-scale reward models demonstrate greater resistance to reward hacking and maintain superior sustained exploration capacity.
items.zh: 这些分析的关键发现包括：i) 大规模奖励模型中的持续探索：26B奖励模型在整个训练过程中表现出显著更大的方差。关键的是，即使在训练后期，这些误差条仍然相当宽，表明模型在后期阶段仍保持显著的探索能力。ii) 较小模型中的加速收敛：相比之下，2B/1B奖励模型的误差条在训练早期就收敛到较窄范围（在后期几乎消失），表明它们的策略更早达到稳定收敛状态并停止有效探索。iii) 正向的扩展-探索相关性：在所有实验条件下，模型规模越大，误差条越宽。这强烈表明大规模奖励模型对奖励破解具有更强的抵抗力，并保持更优越的持续探索能力。

items.capEn: Figure 7: The performance of Seedream and Seedream-Lite models with different RM model sizes. While both models benefit from larger RMs, the performance improvement is substantially more pronounced for the larger model. This suggests that larger generative models require commensurately larger reward models to realize their full potential.
items.capZh: 图7：不同奖励模型规模下Seedream和Seedream-Lite模型的性能。虽然两个模型都受益于更大的奖励模型，但较大模型的性能提升更为显著。这表明较大的生成模型需要相应更大的奖励模型才能充分发挥其潜力。

items.en: DIT Parameter Analysis. Figure 7 presents performance curves for Seedream-3.0 Lite (approximately one-fifth the parameters of Seedream-3.0) and Seedream-3.0 under reward models of varying scales. Key observations include: i) Scaling Laws Apply to Smaller Architectures: Compact DiT models (Seedream-3.0 Lite) also demonstrate scaling phenomena, where performance improves with increasing RM size, consistent with their larger counterparts. ii) Enhanced Scaling Benefits for Larger Models: However, the performance gain rate for Seedream-3.0 Lite does not exceed that of the full-scale model. Specifically, when scaling the RM from 8B to 26B parameters: Seedream-3.0 achieves a performance improvement of +3.92% (81.6% → 84.8%), while Seedream-3.0 Lite exhibits a more modest gain of +2.75% (65.5% → 67.3%). This comparative analysis demonstrates that larger DiT architectures derive greater benefits from reward scaling, yielding more substantial performance improvements.
items.zh: DiT参数分析。图7展示了Seedream-3.0 Lite（参数约为Seedream-3.0的五分之一）和Seedream-3.0在不同规模奖励模型下的性能曲线。关键观察包括：i) 缩放定律适用于较小架构：紧凑型DiT模型（Seedream-3.0 Lite）也表现出扩展现象，即性能随奖励模型规模增大而提升，与其较大版本一致。ii) 较大模型的扩展收益增强：然而，Seedream-3.0 Lite的性能增益率并未超过全尺寸模型。具体而言，当奖励模型从8B扩展到26B参数时：Seedream-3.0实现了+3.92%的性能提升（81.6% → 84.8%），而Seedream-3.0 Lite的增益较为温和，为+2.75%（65.5% → 67.3%）。这一比较分析表明，较大的DiT架构从奖励扩展中获益更多，带来更显著的性能提升。

items.en: Generative Reward Paradigm. We first evaluate the impact of paradigm shift from regression-based to generation-based reward modeling via our 8B RewardDance. As demonstrated in Table 7, transitioning from regressive to generative paradigms consistently improves performance across both FLUX.1-dev (70.8 → 71.6) and Seedream (80.7 → 81.0). Subsequently, incorporating reference images yields further improvements: FLUX.1-dev advances from 71.6 to 73.0, while Seedream progresses from 81.0 to 81.6. These results validate our hypothesis that generative approaches, which align more naturally with VLM architectures, provide a more effective foundation for reward modeling and scaling.
items.zh: 生成式奖励范式。我们首先通过我们的8B RewardDance评估从基于回归到基于生成的奖励建模的范式转变的影响。如表7所示，从回归范式转向生成范式在FLUX.1-dev（70.8 → 71.6）和Seedream（80.7 → 81.0）上均持续提升性能。随后，引入参考图像带来了进一步改进：FLUX.1-dev从71.6提升至73.0，Seedream从81.0提升至81.6。这些结果验证了我们的假设，即生成式方法（与视觉语言模型架构更自然地对齐）为奖励建模和扩展提供了更有效的基础。

items.en: BON Reference Examples. Building upon the generative paradigm, we assess the effectiveness of incorporating Best-of-N (BoN) reference examples for enabling comparative evaluations. Due to computational constraints in image generation, we limit N to a maximum of 16. Table 8 reveals that higher-quality reference images correspond to incremental performance enhancements in Seedream-3.0 (Best-of-16 Top-2 > Best-of-6 Top-2 > Best-of-2 Top-2 > Best-of-16 Bottom-2), where ’Best-of-16 Top-2’ refers to selecting the two best-quality samples from 16 inferred samples using the method described in 3.3.1. This indicates that the quality of the reference images is of critical importance in comparative reward modeling.
items.zh: BON参考示例。在生成范式的基础上，我们评估了引入Best-of-N参考示例以实现比较评估的有效性。由于图像生成中的计算限制，我们将N最大限制为16。表8显示，更高质量的参考图像对应于Seedream-3.0的逐步性能提升（Best-of-16 Top-2 > Best-of-6 Top-2 > Best-of-2 Top-2 > Best-of-16 Bottom-2），其中“Best-of-16 Top-2”指使用3.3.1节所述方法从16个推断样本中选择两个质量最好的样本。这表明参考图像的质量在比较奖励建模中至关重要。

items.en: Reasoning. We isolate the benefits of training with rich, explanatory reasoning data. As shown in Table 9, incorporating CoT data provides substantial performance improvements, elevating the Image-Text Alignment score from 81.6 to 83.6 for Seedream-3.0. This demonstrates that leveraging explicit reasoning pathways enhances the reward model’s capability to generate more accurate and human-aligned judgments.
items.zh: 推理。我们分离了使用丰富解释性推理数据进行训练的好处。如表9所示，引入思维链数据带来了显著的性能提升，将Seedream-3.0的图像-文本对齐分数从81.6提升至83.6。这表明利用显式推理路径增强了奖励模型生成更准确、更符合人类判断的能力。

items.capEn: Figure 8: Text-to-Image generation comparison across reward models of increasing size (Baseline, 1B, 4B, 8B, 26B). Larger reward models demonstrate progressively better prompt adherence, visual quality, and semantic understanding.
items.capZh: 图8：不同规模奖励模型（基线、1B、4B、8B、26B）的文本到图像生成比较。更大的奖励模型在提示遵循、视觉质量和语义理解方面表现出逐步提升。

items.capEn: Figure 9: Video generation quality comparison between 2B and 8B reward models. Top two examples: Image-to-Video generation; Bottom two examples: Text-to-Video generation. The 8B reward model shows improved visual quality and temporal consistency compared to the 2B reward model.
items.capZh: 图9：2B和8B奖励模型之间的视频生成质量比较。上方两个示例：图像到视频生成；下方两个示例：文本到视频生成。与2B奖励模型相比，8B奖励模型在视觉质量和时间一致性方面有所改善。

items.en: Although RewardDance has achieved significant progress on image and video generation tasks, substantial room for exploration remains:
items.zh: 尽管RewardDance在图像和视频生成任务上取得了显著进展，但仍存在大量探索空间：

items.en: Parameter Scaling. The current maximum model size is 26B parameters. Empirical observations suggest that further scaling to larger sizes (e.g., 70B/100B) is likely to yield greater performance gains, which we will investigate in future work.
items.zh: 参数缩放。当前最大模型规模为26B参数。经验观察表明，进一步扩展到更大规模（如70B/100B）可能会带来更大的性能提升，我们将在未来工作中对此进行研究。

items.en: Capability Dimension Scaling. This work focuses mainly on foundational vision language capabilities, such as alignment. Future research will explore other critical dimensions of visual generation, such as motion modeling and aesthetic generation.
items.zh: 能力维度缩放。本工作主要关注基础视觉语言能力，如对齐。未来研究将探索视觉生成的其他关键维度，如运动建模和美学生成。

items.en: Task Scope Scaling. Unified models for joint understanding and generation are a current research hotspot. An important direction is to explore whether Reward Scaling techniques can be effectively applied to such models to synergistically enhance performance across tasks like visual understanding, generation, and editing.
items.zh: 任务范围缩放。用于联合理解和生成的统一模型是当前的研究热点。一个重要方向是探索奖励缩放技术能否有效应用于此类模型，以协同提升视觉理解、生成和编辑等任务的性能。

items.en: Multimodal Scaling. Future many-to-vision tasks (e.g., audio/video-to-video generation) are expected to become mainstream. Consequently, advancing Cross-modal Reward Signal Scaling is a crucial research trend.
items.zh: 多模态缩放。未来的多到视觉任务（如音频/视频到视频生成）预计将成为主流。因此，推进跨模态奖励信号缩放是一个关键的研究趋势。

items.en: Context Scaling. Integrating richer reference information, more complex instructions, reflection capabilities, and in-context learning mechanisms holds promise for further boosting model performance.
items.zh: 上下文缩放。整合更丰富的参考信息、更复杂的指令、反思能力和上下文学习机制有望进一步提升模型性能。

items.en: In this work, we address a critical gap in visual Reward Models (RMs) for diffusion-based generation, where existing methods are constrained by architectural limitations or paradigm mismatches that preclude effective reward scaling. We introduce RewardDance, a scalable RM framework built on a novel generative paradigm. This approach reframes reward prediction as a token generation task by converting reward scores into a Vision-Language Model’s (VLM’s) predicted probability for a "yes" token, which natively aligns with the VLM’s autoregressive mechanism. This core innovation unlocks scaling along two key dimensions: model size (from 1B to 26B parameters) and context richness (task-aware instructions, reference examples, and Chain-of-Thought reasoning). Our comprehensive experiments across text-to-image, text-to-video, and image-to-video tasks demonstrate that scaling along these dimensions consistently improves the quality of the reward signal, which in turn drives stable and significant gains in final generation quality under both RL fine-tuning and Test-time Scaling. Ultimately, our work establishes scalability as a foundational principle for visual RMs, paving the way for more powerful and robust reward modeling in the future of visual generation.
items.zh: 在本工作中，我们解决了扩散生成中视觉奖励模型的一个关键空白，现有方法因架构限制或范式不匹配而无法实现有效的奖励缩放。我们提出了RewardDance，一个基于新颖生成范式的可扩展奖励模型框架。该方法通过将奖励分数转换为视觉语言模型对“是”标记的预测概率，将奖励预测重新定义为标记生成任务，这自然与视觉语言模型的自回归机制对齐。这一核心创新实现了两个关键维度的缩放：模型规模（从1B到26B参数）和上下文丰富度（任务感知指令、参考示例和思维链推理）。我们在文生图、文生视频和图生视频任务上的全面实验表明，沿这些维度缩放一致地提高了奖励信号的质量，进而在强化学习微调和测试时缩放下，稳定且显著地提升了最终生成质量。最终，我们的工作将可扩展性确立为视觉奖励模型的基本原则，为未来视觉生成中更强大、更稳健的奖励建模铺平了道路。
